# Kelmah Frontend 10,000 Item Remediation Backlog (APR 04, 2026)

Purpose: This backlog targets worker-hirer trust, accessibility, mobile reliability, and conversion quality for Kelmah's Ghana-first vocational marketplace.
Method: Items are generated against real frontend module files and mapped to high-impact quality themes used in the ongoing UI and product audits.
Coverage: 327 frontend module files, 32 issue families, 10000 actionable items.

Priority legend: P0 = critical, P1 = high, P2 = medium.

1. [P0] [A11Y_TOUCH] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/admin/pages/PayoutQueuePage.jsx | Fix: Enforce >=44x44 tap targets for every interactive control and preserve visible focus indicators. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah users often rely on low-end phones and thumb-only navigation, so small controls reduce task completion. | Verify: Audit at 320/768 widths for tap-target and focus visibility regressions using strict route captures.
2. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/admin/pages/SkillsAssessmentManagement.jsx | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
3. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/admin/services/adminService.js | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
4. [P1] [STATE_LOADING] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/AuthForm.jsx | Fix: Harden loading states with clear progress feedback and disable duplicate action submissions. | Why for Kelmah: Network variability in target regions makes uncertain loading behavior look like app failure. | Verify: Throttle network and confirm loaders, disabled actions, and retry messaging behave consistently.
5. [P0] [STATE_ERROR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/common/AuthWrapper.jsx | Fix: Normalize friendly error states with direct next-step actions (retry, back, support). | Why for Kelmah: Non-actionable error messages break trust for first-time hirers and workers. | Verify: Force API and timeout failures and confirm every error banner includes a clear recovery path.
6. [P1] [STATE_EMPTY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/common/ProtectedRoute.jsx | Fix: Improve empty states with role-aware guidance and one-click follow-up actions. | Why for Kelmah: Empty results are common in early marketplaces and must still guide users forward. | Verify: Trigger zero-result cases and validate contextual guidance for worker, hirer, and guest users.
7. [P0] [FORM_VALIDATION] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/login/Login.jsx | Fix: Provide inline, field-level validation with examples relevant to Ghana formats and job workflows. | Why for Kelmah: Late-form errors cause abandonment, especially on mobile with expensive data sessions. | Verify: Submit invalid values and confirm immediate, localized, and actionable validation feedback.
8. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/mobile/MobileLogin.jsx | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
9. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/mobile/MobileRegister.jsx | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
10. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/register/Register.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
11. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/constants/commonTrades.js | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
12. [P0] [TRUST_SIGNAL] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/hooks/useAuth.js | Fix: Expose trust signals (verification, ratings, review quality) in a consistent, non-misleading format. | Why for Kelmah: Marketplace trust is critical when hirers select unknown workers remotely. | Verify: Check consistency of badges, rating counts, and verification labels across list/detail surfaces.
13. [P0] [PRICING_CLARITY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/hooks/useRegistrationForm.js | Fix: Clarify pricing semantics (hourly, fixed, escrow, fees) in context before commitment actions. | Why for Kelmah: Ambiguous payment terms create disputes and failed contracts. | Verify: Walk through job apply and contract/payment surfaces to confirm pricing language is unambiguous.
14. [P0] [MESSAGING_USABILITY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/ForgotPasswordPage.jsx | Fix: Improve chat composer ergonomics, attachment feedback, and mobile-safe spacing behavior. | Why for Kelmah: Most negotiations happen in chat, so poor composer UX directly impacts conversion. | Verify: Test mobile keyboard, attachment limits, retry behavior, and quick-reply usability at 320 width.
15. [P1] [NOTIFICATION_ACTION] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/LoginPage.jsx | Fix: Ensure notifications route users to valid destinations with safe fallbacks when targets are stale. | Why for Kelmah: Broken notification links feel unreliable and reduce return engagement. | Verify: Open valid and invalid notifications and confirm deterministic routing and graceful fallback.
16. [P0] [SEARCH_RELEVANCE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/MfaSetupPage.jsx | Fix: Strengthen search/filter relevance with transparent sort explanations and clear active-filter controls. | Why for Kelmah: Discovery is the core of matching workers to hirers; poor relevance kills marketplace utility. | Verify: Evaluate search results under common trade/location queries and inspect active-filter state transitions.
17. [P1] [SEARCH_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/RegisterPage.jsx | Fix: Improve no-result and low-result recovery prompts with broaden-search actions. | Why for Kelmah: Users need immediate guidance when strict filters hide available opportunities. | Verify: Trigger restrictive filters and confirm recovery CTAs broaden results in one step.
18. [P0] [ROLE_GUARD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/RegisterPage.test.jsx | Fix: Audit role-based UI guards so worker/hirer/admin actions are visible only when authorized. | Why for Kelmah: Role leakage confuses users and can expose sensitive actions. | Verify: Sign in as each role and confirm route and action visibility matches authorization rules.
19. [P0] [SECURE_STORAGE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/ResetPasswordPage.jsx | Fix: Review token/session storage handling and eliminate insecure persistence patterns. | Why for Kelmah: Credential leakage in a marketplace app has direct financial and identity risk. | Verify: Inspect auth lifecycle for refresh/logout/state reset behavior across tabs and reloads.
20. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/RoleSelectionPage.jsx | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
21. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/pages/VerifyEmailPage.jsx | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
22. [P2] [ANALYTICS_PATH] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/services/authService.js | Fix: Instrument key funnel events for registration, discovery, messaging, and contract milestones. | Why for Kelmah: Without clean telemetry, product improvements cannot be prioritized reliably. | Verify: Validate event emission, payload hygiene, and de-duplication for major user journeys.
23. [P1] [COPY_LOCALIZATION] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/services/authSlice.js | Fix: Externalize hardcoded strings and prepare locale-safe formatting for date, number, and currency. | Why for Kelmah: Localized wording and formatting reduce cognitive load and user mistakes. | Verify: Run extraction checks and confirm no critical surfaces rely on hardcoded literals.
24. [P2] [MOTION_REDUCED] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/utils/registrationDraftStorage.js | Fix: Respect reduced-motion preferences for all non-essential animations and transitions. | Why for Kelmah: Accessibility compliance requires honoring motion sensitivity preferences. | Verify: Enable reduced-motion and confirm animation-heavy elements degrade gracefully.
25. [P2] [LIST_VIRTUALIZE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/utils/registrationSchema.js | Fix: Virtualize or incrementally render long lists to maintain smooth scrolling performance. | Why for Kelmah: Large worker/job lists can freeze mid-tier devices and degrade trust. | Verify: Load high-volume fixtures and monitor frame drops and memory growth during long scrolls.
26. [P1] [ROUTE_RESILIENCE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/utils/tokenUtils.js | Fix: Harden route boundaries with reliable error fallback, stale-link handling, and recovery actions. | Why for Kelmah: Deep links from search and notifications must not strand users on broken routes. | Verify: Test stale IDs and malformed routes and confirm safe fallback behavior on every protected/public route.
27. [P2] [SKELETON_QUALITY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/calendar/services/calendarSlice.js | Fix: Align skeleton loaders with final layout dimensions to avoid visual jumping. | Why for Kelmah: Poor skeleton fidelity causes disorientation and perceived instability. | Verify: Compare loading and loaded states and ensure shape/spacing continuity.
28. [P2] [FORM_AUTOFILL] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/calendar/services/eventsService.js | Fix: Improve form autocomplete, keyboard type hints, and input mode semantics for mobile. | Why for Kelmah: Faster data entry is critical for users posting jobs or profiles on small screens. | Verify: Validate keyboard modes and autofill behavior across auth/profile/payment forms.
29. [P1] [CARD_HIERARCHY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/calendar/services/eventsService.test.js | Fix: Tighten card information hierarchy so primary action and critical signals appear above the fold. | Why for Kelmah: Users need fast scanning of job/worker cards with minimal cognitive overhead. | Verify: Review card layouts across breakpoints and confirm key metadata is visible without expansion.
30. [P2] [DUPLICATION_REFACTOR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/cards/index.jsx | Fix: Extract repeated UI logic into shared components/hooks to reduce divergence risk. | Why for Kelmah: Duplicated behavior creates subtle inconsistencies in pricing, search, and messaging flows. | Verify: Track duplicate block reduction and verify shared components preserve existing behaviors.
31. [P1] [TEST_COVERAGE_UI] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/cards/JobCard.jsx | Fix: Add high-value interaction tests for route-level success/error/loading and role-specific paths. | Why for Kelmah: Critical user journeys need deterministic regression protection as features evolve quickly. | Verify: Run targeted tests for auth, discovery, messaging, and contracts with both success and failure paths.
32. [P2] [DOCS_OPERABILITY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/cards/UserCard.jsx | Fix: Document component contracts, edge-case behavior, and expected response envelopes near domain modules. | Why for Kelmah: Clear docs reduce onboarding time and prevent accidental behavior regressions. | Verify: Review module docs for completeness against current component props and route contracts.

232. 233. 234. 235. 386. 387. 388. 390. 391. 614. 615. 616. 617. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/PerformanceMetrics.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
618. 619. 621. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/common/Toast.jsx | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
691. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/controls/GestureControl.jsx | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
692. 693. 694. [P2] [MOTION_REDUCED] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/forms/SearchForm.jsx | Fix: Respect reduced-motion preferences for all non-essential animations and transitions. | Why for Kelmah: Accessibility compliance requires honoring motion sensitivity preferences. | Verify: Enable reduced-motion and confirm animation-heavy elements degrade gracefully.
695. [P2] [LIST_VIRTUALIZE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx | Fix: Virtualize or incrementally render long lists to maintain smooth scrolling performance. | Why for Kelmah: Large worker/job lists can freeze mid-tier devices and degrade trust. | Verify: Load high-volume fixtures and monitor frame drops and memory growth during long scrolls.
696. [P1] [ROUTE_RESILIENCE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/index.js | Fix: Harden route boundaries with reliable error fallback, stale-link handling, and recovery actions. | Why for Kelmah: Deep links from search and notifications must not strand users on broken routes. | Verify: Test stale IDs and malformed routes and confirm safe fallback behavior on every protected/public route.
697. [P2] [SKELETON_QUALITY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/layout/index.jsx | Fix: Align skeleton loaders with final layout dimensions to avoid visual jumping. | Why for Kelmah: Poor skeleton fidelity causes disorientation and perceived instability. | Verify: Compare loading and loaded states and ensure shape/spacing continuity.
698. 699. [P1] [CARD_HIERARCHY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/loading/LoadingScreen.jsx | Fix: Tighten card information hierarchy so primary action and critical signals appear above the fold. | Why for Kelmah: Users need fast scanning of job/worker cards with minimal cognitive overhead. | Verify: Review card layouts across breakpoints and confirm key metadata is visible without expansion.
700. [P2] [DUPLICATION_REFACTOR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/components/PageCanvas.jsx | Fix: Extract repeated UI logic into shared components/hooks to reduce divergence risk. | Why for Kelmah: Duplicated behavior creates subtle inconsistencies in pricing, search, and messaging flows. | Verify: Track duplicate block reduction and verify shared components preserve existing behaviors.
701. 702. 703. [P0] [A11Y_TOUCH] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/hooks/useLocalStorage.js | Fix: Enforce >=44x44 tap targets for every interactive control and preserve visible focus indicators. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah users often rely on low-end phones and thumb-only navigation, so small controls reduce task completion. | Verify: Audit at 320/768 widths for tap-target and focus visibility regressions using strict route captures.
704. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/pages/NotFoundPage.jsx | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
705. 706. 707. 709. 710. 711. 712. 774. 1078. 1150. 1151. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/EmojiPicker.jsx | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
1152. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/Message.jsx | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
1153. [P1] [STATE_LOADING] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/MessageAttachments.jsx | Fix: Harden loading states with clear progress feedback and disable duplicate action submissions. | Why for Kelmah: Network variability in target regions makes uncertain loading behavior look like app failure. | Verify: Throttle network and confirm loaders, disabled actions, and retry messaging behave consistently.
1154. [P0] [STATE_ERROR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/MessageDateDivider.jsx | Fix: Normalize friendly error states with direct next-step actions (retry, back, support). | Why for Kelmah: Non-actionable error messages break trust for first-time hirers and workers. | Verify: Force API and timeout failures and confirm every error banner includes a clear recovery path.
1155. [P1] [STATE_EMPTY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/MessageInput.jsx | Fix: Improve empty states with role-aware guidance and one-click follow-up actions. | Why for Kelmah: Empty results are common in early marketplaces and must still guide users forward. | Verify: Trigger zero-result cases and validate contextual guidance for worker, hirer, and guest users.
1156. 1157. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/MessageSearch.jsx | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
1158. 1159. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/messaging/components/common/TypingIndicator.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
1160. 1161. 1303. 1454. 1455. 1456. 1457. 1458. 1459. 1460. 1461. 1462. 1463. 1464. 1534. 1535. 1758. 1759. 1760. 1761. 1762. 1763. 1764. 1765. 1766. 1767. 2443. 2444. 2445. 2446. 2447. 2449. 2450. 2451. 2452. 2453. 2454. 2672. 2673. 2674. 2675. 2676. 2677. 2747. 2748. 2749. 2750. 2752. 2753. 2754. 2755. 2756. 2825. 2826. 2829. 2975. 2977. 2978. 3354. 3355. 3358. 3359. 3360. 3361. 3439. 3658. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/formatters.js | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
3659. 3660. 3661. 3662. 3663. 3664. 3665. 3666. 3668. 3669. 3671. 3672. 4190. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/search/utils/searchOptions.js | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
4191. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/components/common/AccountSettings.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
4192. 4193. 4194. 4195. 4196. 4197. 4198. 4199. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/hirer/components/ProposalReview.jsx | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
4342. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/hirer/components/RecentActivityFeed.jsx | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
4343. 4344. 4345. 4346. 4347. 4348. 4349. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/hirer/pages/HirerProfilePage.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
4350. 4351. 4352. 4646. 4647. 4648. 4649. 4650. 4651. 4652. 4653. 4654. 4655. 4656. 4657. 4658. 4659. 4950. 4951. 4952. 4953. 4954. 4955. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/pages/NotFoundPage.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
4956. 4959. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/hooks/useSettings.js | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
5178. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/pages/SettingsPage.jsx | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
5179. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/services/settingsService.js | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
5180. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/support/pages/HelpCenterPage.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
5181. [P0] [TRUST_SIGNAL] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/support/pages/InfoPage.jsx | Fix: Expose trust signals (verification, ratings, review quality) in a consistent, non-misleading format. | Why for Kelmah: Marketplace trust is critical when hirers select unknown workers remotely. | Verify: Check consistency of badges, rating counts, and verification labels across list/detail surfaces.
5182. 5183. 5184. 5185. 5186. 5187. 5188. 5189. 5190. 5191. 5254. 5255. 5256. 5257. 5258. 5259. 5261. 5265. 5266. 5267. 5336. 5339. 5557. 5558. 5559. 5560. 5561. 5562. 5563. 5564. 5565. 5633. 5634. 5635. 5636. 5637. 5638. 5639. 5640. 5641. 5642. 5643. 5644. 5712. 5713. 5714. 5715. 5716. 5717. 5718. 5719. 5720. 5721. 5785. 5786. 5787. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/pages/NotFoundPage.jsx | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
5937. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/__mocks__/axios.js | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
5938. [P1] [STATE_LOADING] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/appSlice.js | Fix: Harden loading states with clear progress feedback and disable duplicate action submissions. | Why for Kelmah: Network variability in target regions makes uncertain loading behavior look like app failure. | Verify: Throttle network and confirm loaders, disabled actions, and retry messaging behave consistently.
5939. [P0] [STATE_ERROR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/fileUploadService.js | Fix: Normalize friendly error states with direct next-step actions (retry, back, support). | Why for Kelmah: Non-actionable error messages break trust for first-time hirers and workers. | Verify: Force API and timeout failures and confirm every error banner includes a clear recovery path.
5940. [P1] [STATE_EMPTY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/thunkError.js | Fix: Improve empty states with role-aware guidance and one-click follow-up actions. | Why for Kelmah: Empty results are common in early marketplaces and must still guide users forward. | Verify: Trigger zero-result cases and validate contextual guidance for worker, hirer, and guest users.
5941. [P0] [FORM_VALIDATION] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/theme/index.js | Fix: Provide inline, field-level validation with examples relevant to Ghana formats and job workflows. | Why for Kelmah: Late-form errors cause abandonment, especially on mobile with expensive data sessions. | Verify: Submit invalid values and confirm immediate, localized, and actionable validation feedback.
5942. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/theme/tokens.js | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
5943. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/theme/utils.js | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
5944. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/apiUtils.js | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
5945. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/devLogger.js | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
5946. [P0] [TRUST_SIGNAL] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/errorHandler.js | Fix: Expose trust signals (verification, ratings, review quality) in a consistent, non-misleading format. | Why for Kelmah: Marketplace trust is critical when hirers select unknown workers remotely. | Verify: Check consistency of badges, rating counts, and verification labels across list/detail surfaces.
5947. [P0] [PRICING_CLARITY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/formatters.js | Fix: Clarify pricing semantics (hourly, fixed, escrow, fees) in context before commitment actions. | Why for Kelmah: Ambiguous payment terms create disputes and failed contracts. | Verify: Walk through job apply and contract/payment surfaces to confirm pricing language is unambiguous.
5948. 5949. 5950. 5951. 5952. 5953. 6017. 6019. 6165. 6166. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/CertificateUploader.jsx | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
6167. 6168. 6169. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/EarningsReports.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
6170. 6171. 6172. 6173. 6174. 6175. 6176. 6177. [P0] [SECURE_STORAGE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/calendar/services/calendarSlice.js | Fix: Review token/session storage handling and eliminate insecure persistence patterns. | Why for Kelmah: Credential leakage in a marketplace app has direct financial and identity risk. | Verify: Inspect auth lifecycle for refresh/logout/state reset behavior across tabs and reloads.
6241. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/calendar/services/eventsService.js | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
6242. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/calendar/services/eventsService.test.js | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
6243. 6244. 6245. 6246. 6247. 6248. 6249. 6250. 6251. 6252. 6253. 6254. 6255. 6256. 6257. 6545. 6546. 6547. 6548. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/mobile/MobileLogin.jsx | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
6549. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/mobile/MobileRegister.jsx | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
6550. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/components/register/Register.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
6551. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/auth/constants/commonTrades.js | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
6552. 6553. 6554. 6555. 6556. 6557. 6558. 6702. 6703. 6704. 6705. 6706. 6707. 6708. 6709. 6774. 6775. 6863. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/apiUtils.js | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
6926. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/devLogger.js | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
6927. [P1] [STATE_LOADING] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/errorHandler.js | Fix: Harden loading states with clear progress feedback and disable duplicate action submissions. | Why for Kelmah: Network variability in target regions makes uncertain loading behavior look like app failure. | Verify: Throttle network and confirm loaders, disabled actions, and retry messaging behave consistently.
6928. [P0] [STATE_ERROR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/formatters.js | Fix: Normalize friendly error states with direct next-step actions (retry, back, support). | Why for Kelmah: Non-actionable error messages break trust for first-time hirers and workers. | Verify: Force API and timeout failures and confirm every error banner includes a clear recovery path.
6929. [P1] [STATE_EMPTY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/formValidation.js | Fix: Improve empty states with role-aware guidance and one-click follow-up actions. | Why for Kelmah: Empty results are common in early marketplaces and must still guide users forward. | Verify: Trigger zero-result cases and validate contextual guidance for worker, hirer, and guest users.
6930. 6931. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/utils/mediaAssets.js | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
6932. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/contracts/components/common/ContractCard.jsx | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
6933. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/contracts/components/common/ContractForm.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
6934. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/contracts/components/common/ContractsList.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
6935. 7001. 7002. 7003. 7004. 7005. 7006. [P0] [SECURE_STORAGE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/jobs/pages/JobsPage.jsx | Fix: Review token/session storage handling and eliminate insecure persistence patterns. | Why for Kelmah: Credential leakage in a marketplace app has direct financial and identity risk. | Verify: Inspect auth lifecycle for refresh/logout/state reset behavior across tabs and reloads.
7007. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/jobs/services/bidService.js | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
7008. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/jobs/services/jobSlice.js | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
7009. 7010. 7011. 7012. 7086. 7087. 7155. 7156. 7157. 7158. 7229. 7230. 7231. 7232. 7233. 7234. 7235. 7236. 7237. 7238. 7239. 7242. 7243. 7244. 7245. 7246. 7247. 7305. 7306. 7307. 7308. 7309. 7310. 7311. 7312. 7313. 7314. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/search/services/locationService.js | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
7456. 7457. 7458. 7459. [P2] [LIST_VIRTUALIZE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/search/services/smartSearchService.test.js | Fix: Virtualize or incrementally render long lists to maintain smooth scrolling performance. | Why for Kelmah: Large worker/job lists can freeze mid-tier devices and degrade trust. | Verify: Load high-volume fixtures and monitor frame drops and memory growth during long scrolls.
7460. 7461. 7462. 7463. 7466. 7468. 7469. 7532. 7533. 7534. 7535. 7536. 7537. 7538. 7539. 7540. 7541. 7684. 7686. 7691. 7692. 7693. 7694. 7761. 7762. 7763. 7764. 7836. 7842. 7843. 7992. 7993. 7994. 7995. 7996. 7997. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/PortfolioGallery.jsx | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
8140. [P1] [STATE_LOADING] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/PortfolioManager.jsx | Fix: Harden loading states with clear progress feedback and disable duplicate action submissions. | Why for Kelmah: Network variability in target regions makes uncertain loading behavior look like app failure. | Verify: Throttle network and confirm loaders, disabled actions, and retry messaging behave consistently.
8141. [P0] [STATE_ERROR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/ProfileCompletionCard.jsx | Fix: Normalize friendly error states with direct next-step actions (retry, back, support). | Why for Kelmah: Non-actionable error messages break trust for first-time hirers and workers. | Verify: Force API and timeout failures and confirm every error banner includes a clear recovery path.
8142. [P1] [STATE_EMPTY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/ProjectGallery.jsx | Fix: Improve empty states with role-aware guidance and one-click follow-up actions. | Why for Kelmah: Empty results are common in early marketplaces and must still guide users forward. | Verify: Trigger zero-result cases and validate contextual guidance for worker, hirer, and guest users.
8143. 8144. 8145. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/QuickActionsRow.jsx | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
8146. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/RateCalculator.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
8147. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/SkillGapAnalysis.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
8148. 8149. 8150. 8151. 8152. 8216. 8217. 8218. 8219. 8220. 8221. 8223. 8224. 8225. 8226. 8227. 8228. 8229. 8231. 8233. 8234. [P0] [SECURE_STORAGE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/components/common/AccountSettings.jsx | Fix: Review token/session storage handling and eliminate insecure persistence patterns. | Why for Kelmah: Credential leakage in a marketplace app has direct financial and identity risk. | Verify: Inspect auth lifecycle for refresh/logout/state reset behavior across tabs and reloads.
8443. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/components/common/NotificationSettings.jsx | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
8444. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/components/common/PrivacySettings.jsx | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
8445. 8446. 8447. [P2] [MOTION_REDUCED] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/hooks/useSettings.js | Fix: Respect reduced-motion preferences for all non-essential animations and transitions. | Why for Kelmah: Accessibility compliance requires honoring motion sensitivity preferences. | Verify: Enable reduced-motion and confirm animation-heavy elements degrade gracefully.
8448. [P2] [LIST_VIRTUALIZE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/pages/SettingsPage.jsx | Fix: Virtualize or incrementally render long lists to maintain smooth scrolling performance. | Why for Kelmah: Large worker/job lists can freeze mid-tier devices and degrade trust. | Verify: Load high-volume fixtures and monitor frame drops and memory growth during long scrolls.
8449. [P1] [ROUTE_RESILIENCE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/settings/services/settingsService.js | Fix: Harden route boundaries with reliable error fallback, stale-link handling, and recovery actions. | Why for Kelmah: Deep links from search and notifications must not strand users on broken routes. | Verify: Test stale IDs and malformed routes and confirm safe fallback behavior on every protected/public route.
8450. 8451. 8452. 8453. 8454. 8455. 8456. 8457. 8458. 8459. 8598. 8599. 8600. 8601. 8602. 8603. 8606. 8680. 8681. 8682. 8904. 8906. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/Footer.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
8976. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/Header.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
8977. [P0] [TRUST_SIGNAL] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/header/BrandSection.jsx | Fix: Expose trust signals (verification, ratings, review quality) in a consistent, non-misleading format. | Why for Kelmah: Marketplace trust is critical when hirers select unknown workers remotely. | Verify: Check consistency of badges, rating counts, and verification labels across list/detail surfaces.
8978. 8979. 8980. 8981. [P0] [SEARCH_RELEVANCE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/header/NotificationBells.jsx | Fix: Strengthen search/filter relevance with transparent sort explanations and clear active-filter controls. | Why for Kelmah: Discovery is the core of matching workers to hirers; poor relevance kills marketplace utility. | Verify: Evaluate search results under common trade/location queries and inspect active-filter state transitions.
8982. 8983. [P0] [ROLE_GUARD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/header/useAutoHideHeader.js | Fix: Audit role-based UI guards so worker/hirer/admin actions are visible only when authorized. | Why for Kelmah: Role leakage confuses users and can expose sensitive actions. | Verify: Sign in as each role and confirm route and action visibility matches authorization rules.
8984. [P0] [SECURE_STORAGE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/header/UserMenu.jsx | Fix: Review token/session storage handling and eliminate insecure persistence patterns. | Why for Kelmah: Credential leakage in a marketplace app has direct financial and identity risk. | Verify: Inspect auth lifecycle for refresh/logout/state reset behavior across tabs and reloads.
8985. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/Layout.jsx | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
8986. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/layout/components/MobileBottomNav.jsx | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
8987. 8988. 8989. 8990. 8991. [P0] [A11Y_COPY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/RateCalculator.jsx | Fix: Simplify copy to short, plain-language labels and helper text aligned to vocational user comprehension levels. | Why for Kelmah: Kelmah is accessibility-first and serves users with varied literacy, so complex wording creates friction. | Verify: Run UX review of labels and helper text and ensure first-time users can complete key flows without tooltips.
9128. [P0] [A11Y_CONTRAST] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/SkillGapAnalysis.jsx | Fix: Raise contrast for text and action states to WCAG AA in all themes and surfaces. | Why for Kelmah: Users browse outdoors and on lower-brightness devices, making low-contrast content unreadable. | Verify: Check headings, body copy, links, disabled states, and icon-only controls against AA thresholds.
9129. [P1] [STATE_LOADING] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/SkillsAssessment.jsx | Fix: Harden loading states with clear progress feedback and disable duplicate action submissions. | Why for Kelmah: Network variability in target regions makes uncertain loading behavior look like app failure. | Verify: Throttle network and confirm loaders, disabled actions, and retry messaging behave consistently.
9130. [P0] [STATE_ERROR] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/skillsAssessment/styled.js | Fix: Normalize friendly error states with direct next-step actions (retry, back, support). | Why for Kelmah: Non-actionable error messages break trust for first-time hirers and workers. | Verify: Force API and timeout failures and confirm every error banner includes a clear recovery path.
9131. [P1] [STATE_EMPTY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/SkillsVerificationPanel.jsx | Fix: Improve empty states with role-aware guidance and one-click follow-up actions. | Why for Kelmah: Empty results are common in early marketplaces and must still guide users forward. | Verify: Trigger zero-result cases and validate contextual guidance for worker, hirer, and guest users.
9132. [P0] [FORM_VALIDATION] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/TrainingRecommendations.jsx | Fix: Provide inline, field-level validation with examples relevant to Ghana formats and job workflows. | Why for Kelmah: Late-form errors cause abandonment, especially on mobile with expensive data sessions. | Verify: Submit invalid values and confirm immediate, localized, and actionable validation feedback.
9133. [P1] [FORM_RECOVERY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/UserPerformanceDashboard.jsx | Fix: Protect drafts and recover partially completed forms across refresh/navigation events. | Why for Kelmah: Intermittent connectivity and accidental app exits are common in mobile-first usage. | Verify: Interrupt flows intentionally and ensure user progress is restored predictably.
9134. [P1] [PERF_RERENDER] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/WorkerCard.jsx | Fix: Reduce unnecessary rerenders via memoization, stable callbacks, and granular state updates. | Why for Kelmah: Low-memory devices degrade quickly when heavy pages rerender aggressively. | Verify: Profile render counts in React DevTools and confirm meaningful reduction on heavy screens.
9135. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/WorkerFilter.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
9136. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/worker/components/WorkerProfile.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
9137. 9138. 9139. 9140. 9141. 9142. 9204. 9205. 9206. [P0] [ROLE_GUARD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/pages/NotFoundPage.jsx | Fix: Audit role-based UI guards so worker/hirer/admin actions are visible only when authorized. | Why for Kelmah: Role leakage confuses users and can expose sensitive actions. | Verify: Sign in as each role and confirm route and action visibility matches authorization rules.
9207. [P0] [SECURE_STORAGE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/__mocks__/axios.js | Fix: Review token/session storage handling and eliminate insecure persistence patterns. | Why for Kelmah: Credential leakage in a marketplace app has direct financial and identity risk. | Verify: Inspect auth lifecycle for refresh/logout/state reset behavior across tabs and reloads.
9208. [P1] [RETRY_STRATEGY] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/appSlice.js | Fix: Standardize retry logic with exponential backoff and user-visible retry affordances. | Why for Kelmah: Temporary failures should not require full page refreshes for recovery. | Verify: Simulate transient server errors and verify retries are bounded and user-controllable.
9209. [P1] [OFFLINE_GRACE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/services/fileUploadService.js | Fix: Provide offline-aware UI states and queue or defer non-critical actions where possible. | Why for Kelmah: Unstable connectivity is normal for many users and should not erase work or intent. | Verify: Toggle offline mode and confirm banners, disabled actions, and recovery flows are coherent.
9210. 9211. [P1] [COPY_LOCALIZATION] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/theme/index.js | Fix: Externalize hardcoded strings and prepare locale-safe formatting for date, number, and currency. | Why for Kelmah: Localized wording and formatting reduce cognitive load and user mistakes. | Verify: Run extraction checks and confirm no critical surfaces rely on hardcoded literals.
9212. [P2] [MOTION_REDUCED] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/theme/tokens.js | Fix: Respect reduced-motion preferences for all non-essential animations and transitions. | Why for Kelmah: Accessibility compliance requires honoring motion sensitivity preferences. | Verify: Enable reduced-motion and confirm animation-heavy elements degrade gracefully.
9213. [P2] [LIST_VIRTUALIZE] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/common/theme/utils.js | Fix: Virtualize or incrementally render long lists to maintain smooth scrolling performance. | Why for Kelmah: Large worker/job lists can freeze mid-tier devices and degrade trust. | Verify: Load high-volume fixtures and monitor frame drops and memory growth during long scrolls.
9214. 9215. 9216. 9217. 9218. 9219. 9220. 9355. 9356. 9357. 9358. [P1] [PERF_PAYLOAD] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/payment/components/GhanaianMobileMoneyInterface.jsx | Fix: Trim payload and bundle pressure through code-splitting and route-level lazy boundaries. | Why for Kelmah: High bundle weight slows first interaction for users on constrained mobile networks. | Verify: Track route JS sizes and ensure critical paths stay within target performance budgets.
9359. [P1] [PERF_MEDIA] File: kelmah-frontend/src/modules/payment/components/GhanaMobileMoneyPayment.jsx | Fix: Optimize images/media with lazy loading, responsive sizes, and stable skeleton placeholders. | Why for Kelmah: Media-heavy listings can stall scrolling and increase data costs for users. | Verify: Inspect LCP candidates and ensure no layout shifts from delayed media loading.
9360. 9362. 9363. 9364. 9365. 9366. 9367. 9433. 9588. 9589. 9590. 9591. 9592. 9736. 9737. 9738. 9739. 9740. 9741. 9742. 9743. 9744. 9745. 9746. 9747. 9748. 9815. 9819. 9892.
